data.title: FIRST MID BANCSHARES, INC. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
First Mid Bancshares, Inc., a diversified financial services company, has released its Form 10-Q report for the second quarter of 2025. The report highlights the company's financial performance and operational achievements, showcasing growth in key areas and providing insights into its future outlook.

Financial Highlights

  • Total Interest Income: $93.4 million for the three months ended June 30, 2025, reflecting an increase from $88.7 million in the same period of 2024, driven by higher interest and fees on loans.
  • Net Interest Income: $63.9 million for the three months ended June 30, 2025, up from $56.8 million in the same period of 2024, indicating improved interest income performance.
  • Net Income: $23.4 million for the three months ended June 30, 2025, compared to $19.7 million in the same period of 2024, showcasing a positive growth in profitability.
  • Basic Net Income Per Common Share: $0.98 for the three months ended June 30, 2025, up from $0.83 in the same period of 2024, reflecting enhanced earnings per share.
  • Diluted Net Income Per Common Share: $0.98 for the three months ended June 30, 2025, compared to $0.82 in the same period of 2024, indicating improved diluted earnings per share.

Business Highlights

  • Segment Reporting: The company operates as a single segment entity for financial reporting purposes, focusing on consolidated performance across its subsidiaries, including First Mid Bank & Trust, First Mid Wealth Management Company, First Mid Insurance Group, and First Mid Captive, Inc.
  • Geographical Performance: The company's business activities are concentrated in Illinois, Missouri, Texas, and Wisconsin, with a significant portion of the loan portfolio secured by collateral located within these regions.
  • Agricultural Loans: The company has a substantial exposure to the agricultural sector, with $687.8 million in loans to borrowers related to agriculture, particularly in corn and other grain farming, which increased by $76.9 million from the previous year.
  • Commercial Real Estate Loans: The company maintains a diverse commercial real estate loan portfolio, including loans to shopping centers, industrial and warehouse properties, hotels, and other property types, with a total of $2.4 billion in commercial real estate loans.
  • Loan Portfolio Diversification: The company's loan portfolio is diversified across various sectors, including commercial and industrial loans, consumer loans, and loans secured by real estate, with a focus on maintaining sound underwriting standards.
  • Allowance for Credit Losses: The company has a disciplined credit review process to maintain an adequate allowance for credit losses, considering factors such as historical net loan losses, economic conditions, and industry trends.
  • Future Outlook: The company anticipates stable to declining interest rates, which may positively impact the commercial and industrial loan segments, and continues to monitor economic conditions and regulatory changes that could affect its operations.

SEC Filing: FIRST MID BANCSHARES, INC. [ FMBH ] - 10-Q - Aug. 08, 2025

data.title: TSX set for biggest weekly gain in 11 months; earnings, fed revamp in focus
data.description:
Canada's main stock index inched up on Friday and was on track for its largest weekly gain in 11 months as investors evaluated corporate results, while U.S. President Donald Trump's Federal Reserve revamp fueled hopes for rate cuts.

The S&P/TSX composite index rose 0.2% to 27,816.03 points by 09:55 a.m. ET (1455 GMT), after posting record highs this week.

"The TSX has been benefiting this week from a generally positive response to earnings that have been coming out in Canada. Shopify was one of the biggest weighted stocks in Canada and it went up 20% a couple of days ago so that alone boosted the Canadian market," said Colin Cieszynski, chief market strategist at SIA Wealth Management.

Gold mining stocks (.SPTTGD) gained on Friday, with miners Lundin Gold and Sandstorm Gold rising 7.5% and 2.3%, respectively.

Among individual stocks, OpenText gained 7.8% after the software company's fourth-quarter revenue beat expectations.

Energy Fuels' shares jumped 7.4% and were among the top gainers after second-quarter results.

Conversely, Sun Life Financial dropped 7.7% and was the top laggard on the index despite posting a rise in second-quarter profit on Thursday.

Meanwhile, data showed that the Canadian economy shed thousands of jobs in July, while the unemployment rate remained steady at 6.9%.

"We had a job decline in Canada which, combined with the disappointing U.S. numbers from last week, suggests the North American employment market is slowing and that the economy itself might be starting to slow," Cieszynski added.

Trump's nomination of Council of Economic Advisers Chairman Stephen Miran to serve the final months of a newly vacant seat fueled hopes for a more dovish policy ahead.

The White House is seeking a permanent addition to the governing board as well as a new Fed Chair to replace Jerome Powell after his term ends in May 2026.

data.title: Russian rouble weakens against dollar on uncertainty over US-Russia talks
data.description:
The Russian rouble weakened against the U.S. dollar on Friday on uncertainty surrounding a possible meeting between U.S. President Donald Trump and Russian President Vladimir Putin.

By 1350 GMT the rouble was down 0.7% at 79.82 to the dollar, according to data compiled by LSEG based on over-the-counter quotes. The rouble was down 0.9% against China's yuan, the most traded foreign currency in Russia.

Putin and Trump are expected to meet in the coming days, the Kremlin said on August 7, as the U.S. president seeks a breakthrough to end the Ukraine war, but no venue and firm date have yet been set for the meeting.

The rouble strengthened by 1% on the news about the meeting but then started to weaken.

"Forex rates yesterday fell to two-week lows, driven by a surge of optimism regarding geopolitics. We do not yet see sufficient grounds, given the low oil prices and seasonality, to lower our target range," said Evgeny Loktykhov, an analyst at PSB.

** Shares of Under Armour slump as much as 21% to $5.25 in early trading

8 August 2025

Announcement of SARB Approval

Following the successful non-pre-emptive placing of a total of 48,253,994 ordinary shares of 5 pence each (the "Placing Shares") in the capital of the Company as announced on 31 July 2025, Hammerson is pleased to announce that the Financial Surveillance Department of the South African Reserve Bank has given its requisite approval to inward list the Placing Shares.

Applications will be made for: (i) the 16,173,604 placing shares placed with placees who are South African Qualifying Investors (the "SA Placing Shares") to be admitted to (a) listing in the Equity Shares (Commercial Companies) category of the Official List of the Financial Conduct Authority and to trading on the main market for listed securities of the London Stock Exchange plc; and (b) listing on the Official List of The Irish Stock Exchange plc, trading as Euronext Dublin and to trading on the main market for listed securities of Euronext Dublin; and (ii) for the Placing Shares to be admitted to listing and trading as a secondary inward listing on the Main Board of the securities exchange operated by the JSE Limited ("Second Admission") as soon as reasonably practicable.
